attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. <observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Dual-channel streaming architecture for Telegram draft messages</title>
  <subtitle>Split draft frames and tool progress into separate channels to eliminate animation jitter in Telegram's append-only draft model</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Telegram draft updates use pure append-only animation (fade-in trailing characters only), causing jitter when mid-text changes occur</fact>
    <fact>Tool progress indicators (emoji rotations, second counters, sliding windows) are mid-text mutations incompatible with append-only semantics</fact>
    <fact>renderDraftReply now handles only header + body + cursor as pure append-only content</fact>
    <fact>renderStatusMessage sends tool progress and thinking preview in a separate silent message, updated via editMessageText</fact>
    <fact>Status messages are always deleted in finally blocks (cancel/superseded/error paths)</fact>
    <fact>Draft is recreated after status message creation to handle TTL semantics correctly</fact>
    <fact>Implementation spans 13 commits from e3a3a45 through 136f0c0, touching 8 source files</fact>
    <fact>Smoke tests check-draft-render, check-streaming-token-strip, check-editnow-race, and check-ab7-goal-loop all pass</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Telegram's draft message API uses an append-only animation model that fades in new trailing characters. Tool progress indicators require mid-text mutations (emoji rotations, second updates, sliding position windows), causing visual jitter when rendered in draft frames. The solution splits streaming into two independent channels: renderDraftReply handles pure append-only content (header, body, cursor), while renderStatusMessage sends tool progress and thinking previews in a separate silent message that updates via standard editMessageText (no append-only constraint). Status messages are created with mute flag on first content, updated in-place for subsequent ticks, and always deleted in finally blocks regardless of exit path. Draft recreation after status message creation ensures TTL semantics remain correct. This architecture eliminates jitter while preserving real-time progress feedback, verified through four smoke test suites covering draft rendering, token stripping, edit races, and goal loops.</narrative>

The update documents two major deliverables: rich rendering support (already operational via telegram-rich-renderer.ts) and Draft API streaming (e3a3a45, July 10, enabled by default for private chats). Benefits are reframed from aspirational to delivered, and remaining limitations (429 rate limits, private-chat-only scope) are repositioned from blockers to architectural boundaries. This marks the proposal's graduation from "P2 large task requiring /dev-design" to production-ready feature.</narrative>

7. Telegram Rich Messages 升級 核心已完成

\\n

Telegram Bot API 10.1（2026-06-11）新增 Rich Messages。

\\n\\n

新功能

\\n
    \\n
  • 標題（heading）
    • \\n
  • 表格（table）
    • \\n
  • 清單（ordered/unordered list）
    • \\n
  • LaTeX 數學式
    • \\n
  • 摺疊區塊（spoiler/expandable）
    • \\n
  • 腳註（footnote）
    • \\n
  • 字元上限提升至 32768
    • \\n
\\n\\n

現況（2026-07-12 更新：draft 化阻塞已解）

\\n
    \\n
  • 已落地：rich 渲染（表格/程式碼高亮/LaTeX）——editMessageText + rich_message（Bot API 10.1 官方參數），telegram-rich-renderer.ts 走此路徑；失敗自我收斂修復見 ce0e1ac。
    • \\n
  • 已落地：Draft API streaming 路徑（e3a3a45，2026-07-10）——sendMessageDraft / sendRichMessageDraft，預設開啟（TG_DRAFT_ENABLED=1，private chat）。
    • \\n
  • 阻塞已解：原判斷 append-only 累積（yield 出去的字收不回）與 tool 行 mid-text 更新（emoji 翻轉/秒數/滑動視窗）不相容，需重寫 hold-back incremental emitter 才能解。實際採用更輕量的雙通道拆分（136f0c0，2026-07-12）：renderDraftReply 只回 header+body+cursor（純 append-only）；tool 進度/thinking 拆到獨立 renderStatusMessage，走一般訊息 sendMessage（靜音）+ editMessageText 原地整則重繪，不進 draft 通道。根治動畫抖動，且不需要 /dev-design 大改設計。
    • \\n
\\n\\n

Draft 化已兌現的效益

\\n
    \\n
  • Draft 動畫 UX：ephemeral draft 連續打字效果，免 placeholder 先發後改；雙通道拆分後動畫不再抖動。
    • \\n
  • 半截 token 洩漏消失：draft 通道只含已定形的 header+body+cursor。
    • \\n
  • 429 結構性減壓：draft 幀可跳過不丟資料。
    • \\n
\\n\\n
\\n 仍不會得到的：final send 照樣受 429（「draft 不受限流」是誤解，plugin 依賴 autoRetry）；draft 僅限 private chat，relay/group/失敗 fallback 仍走 plain path，format-html escape 不能全刪。這些是架構既定邊界，非待辦。\\n
\\n
